items.text: BranchHandle type holds the fork-point root hash, current root hash, and per-shard WAL buffer references.
fork() records the current root hash as the fork-point and allocates empty per-shard WAL buffers without copying any tree nodes.
Fork completes in O(1) time regardless of database size (CN1).
A forked branch can be read and written independently of the parent — mutations on one do not affect the other.
Branch reads check the branch's WAL buffer first, then fall through to the shared content-addressed tree.
Fork operates per-shard: each shard's tree gets an independent fork-point and branch WAL buffer (CN5).
merge() takes two diverged root hashes and their common ancestor and produces a merged tree.
Merge skips subtrees where all three hashes (ancestor, parent, branch) are identical (CN2).
Clean merges (only one side changed relative to ancestor) are applied automatically without invoking conflict resolution.
True conflicts (both sides changed the same key relative to ancestor) are routed to the conflict resolution policy.
Merge produces its output as a single batch commit to the tree — one path-to-root rewrite, not per-entry rewrites.
Merge operates per-shard: each shard's tree is merged independently (CN5).
LWW policy resolves conflicts by taking the branch value over the parent value.
Vector-clock policy tracks per-key version vectors and distinguishes true conflicts (concurrent) from dominated updates (one causally after the other).
True conflicts under vector-clock policy are surfaced to the caller as unresolved — they are not silently dropped (CN3).
Custom merge function receives (key, ancestor_value, parent_value, branch_value) and returns the resolved value.
Conflict resolution policy is specified at merge time, not at fork time.
Snapshot registry maintains a mapping from human-readable names to root hashes.
Snapshot registry maintains a commit log of all root hashes in commit order.
snapshot(name) records the current committed root hash under the given name.
checkout(root_hash) opens a read-only view at the specified root hash without WAL replay (CN6).
Read-only checkout view supports get and range operations but rejects put/delete/commit.
Snapshot listing returns all named snapshots with their root hashes and commit timestamps.
prune(name) removes a named snapshot from the registry.
Pruning walks the node store's reference graph to identify nodes no longer referenced by any active branch or named snapshot.
Pruning never deletes nodes referenced by an active branch or named snapshot (CN4).
Pruning reports the number of nodes and bytes reclaimed.
